---
title: "Figure 5 (macro_IR) — information elasticity in channel number: concentrate vs spread"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Orthogonal to the interval analysis. Discrete derivative of the per-recording parameter INFORMATION
(1/variance) with respect to channel number:
    alpha = d log(info) / d log(N_ch) = - [log var(N') - log var(N)] / [log N' - log N]
between consecutive N_ch (normalised by the log-ratio, so uneven steps are comparable). More channels
always help (alpha > 0); the design question is 2N-once vs N-twice, i.e. whether info(2N) > 2 info(N):
    alpha > 1  -> super-linear -> concentrating (2N once) beats spreading (N twice)
    alpha = 1  -> linear -> indifferent
    alpha < 1  -> sub-linear -> SPREADING wins (two recordings of N beat one of 2N)
The concentrate-vs-spread factor for an exact doubling is 2^(alpha-1). macro_IR, noise 0.1, 1c2ae6f,
Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance diagonal, all params. Plotted vs the geometric midpoint of each N_ch step,
coloured by interval; reference at alpha = 1.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
COV      <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

d <- map_dfr(Sys.glob("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_0.1_battery_pool_G.csv"),
             ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

v <- d %>%
  filter(component_path == COV, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)]) %>%
  group_by(interval_in_tau, param_name, Num_ch) %>%
  summarise(var = mean(value), .groups = "drop")

dd <- v %>% group_by(interval_in_tau, param_name) %>% arrange(Num_ch) %>%
  mutate(N_next = lead(Num_ch), var_next = lead(var),
         k = N_next / Num_ch,                          # actual increment of this step (2, 2.5, ...)
         L = k * var_next / var,                       # covariance loss vs linear: 1 = no loss, >1 = loss
         N_mid = sqrt(Num_ch * N_next)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(L)) %>% ungroup() %>%
  mutate(iv_f = factor(interval_in_tau),
         param = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.5, fig.height = 5.6}
g <- ggplot(dd, aes(N_mid, L, colour = iv_f, group = iv_f)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey45", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, nrow = 2, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(10, 100, 1000, 10000), labels = c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4")) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = expression(Delta / tau[min]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  labs(x = expression(N[ch]~"(start of the step)"),
       y = "covariance lost by joining   L = (N'/N)*cov(N')/cov(N)   (1 = no loss)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_IR_channel_loss.pdf", g, width = 7.5, height = 5.6)
```

```{r table}
# alpha (median over intervals) and the concentrate-vs-spread factor 2^(alpha-1); <1 => spread wins
dd %>% group_by(param_name, step = paste0(Num_ch, "->", N_next, " (x", round(k, 1), ")")) %>%
  summarise(L = round(median(L), 2), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(param_name, step) %>% as.data.frame()
```
